notes: PRS-standard pan-tilt bipod with single-pull leg deployment. ARCA / Picatinny clamp. The Ckye-Pod has been the dominant PRS bipod 2022 onward.
Extended-range Ckye-Pod with double-pull telescoping legs for greater height range. Same pan-tilt head and ARCA / Picatinny clamp as the single-pull.
Ultra-low prone bipod. Non-folding fixed-height legs designed for the lowest possible position. Pan-tilt head, ARCA / Picatinny clamp.
Mid-tier MDT bipod positioned below the Ckye-Pod. Pan-tilt head, ARCA / Picatinny clamp, folding legs.
The original Atlas bipod. Direct Picatinny mount (no clamp), pan-tilt head with cant lock, five leg positions. Long-standing precision-rifle standard.
Atlas BT10 with the LW17 lever-style Picatinny clamp for tool-less attach / detach. Same pan-tilt head and leg geometry as the BT10-NC.
Tall PSR variant designed for prone over uneven terrain. Extended legs with five positions, LW17 lever clamp, pan-tilt head.
Atlas CAL (Cant And Loc) variant. Pan-tilt with a positive cant lever to lock the rifle level under load. LW17 lever clamp.
The classic Harris 6 to 9 inch swivel bipod (the M designates the swivel / pan model). Sling-stud mount, notched telescoping legs, decades-long varmint and hunting standard.
Harris 9 to 13 inch swivel bipod. Same notched telescoping legs as the S-BRM but extended for sitting / kneeling positions.
Harris 13.5 to 27 inch tall swivel bipod for sitting / kneeling shots. Sling-stud mount, notched telescoping legs.
Fixed (non-swivel) tall Harris bipod. The C suffix indicates the spring-tensioned leg-extension design. Sling-stud mount.
Flat-clamp Picatinny mount, four leg positions, integral pan-tilt with locking cant. Built in Idaho. Heavier and stiffer than an Atlas.
Heavier Bolt-Action Rifle bipod with five leg positions and quick-detach Picatinny clamp. The G2 update added an improved cant-lock.
Heavy-duty bipod designed for .50 BMG and large-caliber rifles. Wider stance, beefier legs, locking cant and pan.
Compact Small-Rifle bipod for AR-platform and lighter precision rigs. Five leg positions, integrated cant-lock pan-tilt.
Magpul's polymer-and-aluminum bipod. The Sling Stud SKU plus M-LOK, Picatinny, and ARCA / RRS-spec variants share the leg unit. Pan-tilt head with integrated cant-lock.
Magnetic-attach hunting bipod. Carbon-fiber fixed legs with a rare-earth magnet stem; the Javelin adapter replaces the rifle's sling-stud. Removable in seconds.
Standard-length Javelin. Same magnetic stem and Spartan adapter as the Lite, with longer carbon-fiber legs for taller cover or sloped ground.
Telescoping carbon-fiber hunting bipod with Spartan magnetic-stem attachment plus ARCA option. Quick height adjustment, pan-tilt head, lighter than most metal alternatives.
PRS-oriented telescoping bipod with rear-tension knob, pan-tilt head, integrated ARCA / Picatinny clamp. Made in the USA.
Tall variant of the Phoenix bipod for uneven terrain or tall grass. Same head and clamp design as the standard, with extended telescoping legs.
Heavy-Cant Tactical bipod with locking cant, pan, and twin tension knobs for loading the bipod under recoil. ARCA / Picatinny clamp.
Shorter-leg HCT for low-prone or carbine work. Same pan-tilt head and tension knobs as the full HCT, lighter and shorter overall.
Wiebad's PRS bipod with locking pan-tilt and integrated tension control. ARCA / Picatinny clamp; positioned against the Ckye-Pod.
Compact FrontEnder for low-prone positions. Same pan-tilt head and clamp as the standard, with shorter legs.
Aftermarket lever upgrade for Harris-pattern bipods that locks pan-cant tension. Sold as a complete bipod or a retrofit kit; the Pod-Loc lever has been an F-Class / tactical staple for decades.
Swedish-made bipod with three locked leg positions, integrated cant-lock and rear pan, machined from billet. Picatinny mount with optional ARCA adapter.
